articles.title: Properties near marathon routes listed across England
articles.summary: Five residential properties located along marathon and half-marathon routes across England have been listed for sale, with prices ranging from £350,000 to £1.6 million. The listings span from Brighton to Yorkshire, including properties in London's Dalston and Woolwich areas. The post Properties near marathon routes listed across England appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows
articles.summary: The proportion of landlords selling former rental properties has fallen by almost half over the past year, according to new data from TwentyCi. The share of homes coming to market that were previously rented dropped from 22.5% in Q1 2025 to 12.4% in Q1 2026, representing a year-on-year reduction of 45%. The post Landlord property sales decline 45% as rental exodus slows appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses
articles.summary: A coalition of investors and freeholders has launched a campaign to challenge leasehold reforms, claiming losses could exceed £30 billion. The group, Justice for Property Rights, is seeking legal advice on bringing a collective action before the European Court of Human Rights. The post Investor group challenges leasehold reforms over £30bn losses appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange
articles.summary: Property transactions in the UK are taking significantly longer to complete, with 43% of housing sales now requiring more than 17 weeks to reach exchange. The trade body described the figure as the highest level recorded, highlighting ongoing inefficiencies in the transaction process. The post Property transactions take over 17 weeks to exchange appeared first on PropertyWire.

articles.title: UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026
articles.summary: UK property sales have declined 7.6% year-to-date compared to 2025, with 342,000 homes sold subject to contract by mid-April 2026. The figures show transactions remain below last year's levels but continue to outperform 2024 by 5.6% and sit 12.9% above pre-Covid norms. The post UK property transactions down 7.6% year-on-year in 2026 appeared first on PropertyWire.
